response.justification: Talented engineer with strong distributed systems and infrastructure experience, but fundamentally misaligned with role requirements. The position requires 8 years of C++ (minimum qualification), while the resume documents only ~6 months of explicit C++ work at National Instruments. Additionally, lacks evidence of 2+ years in formal technical leadership mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. While the candidate excels in software craftsmanship and distributed systems architecture, the C++ experience gap is a critical blocker for a technical lead position overseeing a cloud compute platform.
strengths: Strong infrastructure and distributed systems background: microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second, Kubernetes/AWS experience, GitLab/CI/CD infrastructure setup
Deep commitment to testing and engineering best practices: TDD across multiple roles, organized Refactoring Dojos/Code Katas, established patterns and standards across organizations
Demonstrated ownership of large, complex technical projects: GISCollective platform design and maintenance, full-stack product launches, top-10 Apple TV app development
concerns: Critical gap in C++ experience: position requires 8 years minimum, resume shows ~6 months at National Instruments plus academic projects; career primarily focused on JavaScript/TypeScript
No evidence of 2+ years in formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers; current Staff role only 1 month old, previous roles were individual contributor at senior level
Limited depth of production cloud architecture experience: AWS CDK and Kubernetes mentioned but not deeply detailed, no explicit examples of balancing short/long-term platform needs at scale
